# Questions in Livestream 176

Live Stream #176: The People Groups of YouTube

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=494" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">8:14</a> | In your novels, do you focus more on world-building or on character? | He aims for a balance, but says his fiction usually leans more toward strong character work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=718" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:58</a> | Are angels, powers, and spirit creatures also children of God, and does God love them as he loves humanity? | He says they are God's spiritual creatures and are loved, but not in the same redemptive way as humans. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=838" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">13:58</a> | Can Christian authors write mythic fiction about angels, origins, and paradise using non-biblical imagery? | Yes, provided the theology and worldview stay straight and the fiction does not contradict biblical revelation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=1077" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:57</a> | What do you think of the Catholic Church's process for verifying miracle claims? | He likes the caution and says the process tends to avoid declaring certainty too quickly. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=1629" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">27:09</a> | What are your thoughts on Michael Heiser's book *Angels*? | He has only skimmed it, but says it did not seem as strong as *The Unseen Realm*. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=1694" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:14</a> | How do you resolve the tension between theological retrieval and Heiser's claim that the Bible's context is not church history? | He distinguishes christotelic reading from later christocentric retrieval and says the original context should remain the starting point. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=2252" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">37:32</a> | How do we know a Dead Sea Scroll fragment is the lost Book of the Wars of Yahweh? | He is uncertain, but recalls that matching quotations in other texts may have been used to identify it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=2512" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:52</a> | If blessings are a big deal, why do we bless food? | He says it is really a prayer of thanks rather than literally making the food holy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=2848" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">47:28</a> | What are your thoughts on Keil and Delitzsch's Exodus commentary? | He is not a fan, especially where it handles Egyptian material poorly. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=3094" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:34</a> | Where did the myth that Job is the oldest biblical book come from? | He is not sure, but says serious scholars no longer treat Job as the oldest book. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=3342" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">55:42</a> | Is partialism only a heresy if you believe in divine simplicity? | He says divine simplicity and the essence-energies distinction are not necessarily mutually exclusive, though divine simplicity has other issues. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=3485" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:05</a> | What does John Walton mean by functional creation in Genesis? | He says Walton reads Genesis as presenting creation in terms of function and form rather than material origins alone. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=4083" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:08:03</a> | Are the Horites in Genesis 14:6 Hurrians, or is this an anachronism? | He says Horites and Hurrians are different groups; the Horites are tied to Mount Seir and possibly the Shasu. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=4395" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:13:15</a> | Could first-century Judeans have spoken Greek, and why are some sayings preserved in other languages? | He says ancient polyglotism was normal, especially in crossroads communities. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=4660" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:17:40</a> | How can scholars better fight misinformation and non-scholarly myths online? | He says the channel spends much of its time doing exactly that, though the problem can feel overwhelming. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=4981" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:23:01</a> | Is Moloch an ancient demonic god or a ritual fire sacrifice? | He says the latter is more likely, because evidence for Moloch as a worshiped deity is lacking. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=5126" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:25:26</a> | What do you think about the tradition that John survived being plunged into boiling oil? | He says it could explain John's exile, but the solidity of the tradition is hard to determine. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=5230" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:10</a> | Was Muhammad given a false revelation by a demon, or was he lying? | He avoids speculating directly and says Islamic revelation contradicts biblical revelation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=5372" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:32</a> | What is the biblically accurate process of getting married? | He says there is no prescriptive process, though Israelite marriage involved legal and contractual negotiation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=5749" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:35:49</a> | Were ancient marriage consummation customs a throwback to fertility pagan rites? | He says no; he treats them as pragmatic and contractual safeguards. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=5900" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:38:20</a> | What if a woman broke her hymen before marriage through sports or another cause? | He says that was less of an issue in the ancient world because women were not usually playing sports in that way. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=6660" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:51:00</a> | Is an upside-down cross evil? | No. He says it is traditionally the cross of St. Peter and originally symbolized humility, not evil. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=7249" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:00:49</a> | Would you commission an official Dr. Falk chronology-of-the-Exodus infographic poster? | He is doubtful because the merch store gets little traffic and posters have not sold well. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=8456" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:20:56</a> | Did any nation other than Israel have prophecies about unbelieving nations being blessed through them? | He says he does not know of a close parallel and treats Israel as unusual on that point. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=8708" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:25:08</a> | How should we use sources like Nuzi and Amarna responsibly in exegesis? | He says to use them as historical sources with caution, accounting for genre, rhetoric, and possible hyperbole. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=9419" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:36:59</a> | How do practitioners of Orthodox Judaism usually interpret the seed promise in Genesis 22:18? | He says they usually read the seed as the nation of Israel rather than as a messianic individual. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=9552" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:39:12</a> | What do you think of the age variations in Genesis 5 and 11 between the Septuagint, Samaritan Pentateuch, and Masoretic Text? | He first clarifies the textual abbreviations and treats the issue as a textual-tradition question. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=10521" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:55:21</a> | What does "righteous" mean in the Old Testament, for example in Genesis 18:23? | He says it does not simply mean sinless, but concerns a broader covenantal and moral category. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=11915" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:18:35</a> | Is there proof the peace symbol was ever meant for demonic use? | He says no, describing it instead as a long-lived symbol later misused by others. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=12789" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:33:09</a> | Which king was the Exodus pharaoh if Ramesses II's mummy is in a museum? | He still identifies Ramesses II, saying the Bible does not require the pharaoh to have drowned in the sea. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=13009" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:36:49</a> | Why are unmarried couples who have sex sinful, and how does this show marriage is more than a legal certificate? | He says it violates marriage's covenantal purpose and can function as a mock or test marriage. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=13147" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:39:07</a> | Why was the battle over the body of Moses left out of the Old Testament? | He says it is an ideological or heavenly-realm event, not a Torah narrative event. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=13332" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:42:12</a> | How has the Christian faith been good for the world? | He gives examples including women's consent in marriage and periods of social peace and institution-building. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=13674" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:47:54</a> | Could a body found in an Egyptian tomb have been a later local or tomb robber rather than the original burial? | Yes. He says locals sometimes reused tombs for unrelated burials, so later intrusion is possible. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=14425" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:00:25</a> | What are your thoughts on Christian nationalism? | He strongly rejects it, defining it as a distorted mix of Old Testament law-as-state-law and racism rather than ordinary Christian civic duty. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=15509" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:18:29</a> | Were there Freemasons on the King James Bible editing board? | He says the verifiable history of Freemasonry is too late to make that likely. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sKKScMy3Q64?t=15841" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:24:01</a> | How can you have confidence as a Christian? | He says doubt remains part of Christian life, but Scripture, prayer, and faithful church practice can steady and reduce it. |
